skill.name: senior-devops
skill.description: DevOps guidance and helper scripts for CI/CD planning, Terraform scaffolding, deployment workflows, and operational checks.
skill.summary: Plan pipelines, infrastructure automation, and deployment workflows with DevOps reference material and local helper scripts.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code fences, generic words such as "Description", and DevOps command examples. The confirmed concern is that all three Python helper scripts can write JSON output to a user-supplied path, which is legitimate but should be used with care.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
medium_findings.description: The Python helper scripts accept an output file path and write JSON results there. This is expected CLI behavior, but it can overwrite writable files if the user supplies a sensitive path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is directly present and controlled by the --output argument. The risk is bounded because it only writes generated JSON locally and does not include network exfiltration or privilege escalation.

medium_findings.title: Operational Command Examples Require User Review
medium_findings.description: The skill documents commands for package installation, Docker, Docker Compose, and Kubernetes. These are not executed by the skill, but users should review targets and credentials before running infrastructure commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documentation examples, so there is no automatic command execution. DevOps commands can still affect local or cloud resources if a user runs them without review.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Hits Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences and usage snippets. I did not find Ruby backtick execution or programmatic shell execution at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell interpolation. The only executable files are Python scripts, and they do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or exec.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match generic words such as "Description" and "Comprehensive" rather than MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted review found no weak cryptographic algorithm names or crypto library calls. The detected lines are prose descriptions and argparse descriptions.

low_findings.title: Environment File Static Hit Is Setup Documentation
low_findings.description: The environment file finding is a command showing how to copy an example environment file. It does not read secrets, transmit data, or instruct the model to expose environment values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a setup snippet that copies .env.example to .env. There is no code path that opens existing environment files or exports secret values.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are Section Headings
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections point to anti-pattern headings and general documentation. No evidence found of host fingerprinting, process enumeration, network scanning, or credential discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are labels and bullets in documentation. The Python scripts only validate whether a target path exists and record the path string.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Write Via --output
dangerous_patterns.description: Each helper script writes a JSON report to the path provided through --output. This pattern should be documented because it can modify local files chosen by the user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly open the requested output path in write mode. The behavior is transparent and local, so it is a warning rather than a blocker.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable deployment guidance without rebuilding DevOps checklists from scratch. This skill provides CI/CD, Terraform, deployment, and operations references for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Provides CI/CD pipeline planning guidance and command examples.
Includes Terraform and infrastructure-as-code reference material.
Documents deployment workflow concepts and operational practices.
Supplies Python helper scripts for simple target analysis reports.
Can produce JSON summaries from local script runs when requested.
limitations: Reference documents are generic and contain placeholder examples.
Scripts perform minimal analysis and do not generate complete production infrastructure.
Cloud provider setup still requires user credentials, policy review, and manual validation.
The helper scripts can overwrite files when given an existing output path.

use_cases.title: Design a CI/CD rollout
use_cases.description: Create a practical pipeline checklist for builds, tests, deployment gates, and release verification.

use_cases.title: Review infrastructure automation
use_cases.description: Use the Terraform guidance to organize modules, review quality checks, and identify missing operational controls.

use_cases.title: Prepare deployment runbooks
use_cases.description: Draft deployment steps, rollback notes, monitoring checks, and troubleshooting actions for application releases.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner pipeline checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a first CI/CD plan for a service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this DevOps skill to create a basic CI/CD checklist for a web service. Include build, test, deployment, rollback, and monitoring steps.

prompt_templates.title: Terraform review guide
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to improve an infrastructure-as-code repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Terraform project structure using this skill. Identify missing modules, environment separation, state management concerns, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Deployment runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a deployment runbook for a containerized application. Include preflight checks, deployment commands to review, verification steps, rollback triggers, and owner handoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced platform audit
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a broad DevOps maturity assessment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this platform using the skill references. Cover CI/CD controls, Terraform practices, Kubernetes deployment safety, secrets handling, observability, and release risk.

output_examples.input: Create a CI/CD plan for a Node.js API.
output_examples.output: A structured release checklist covering dependency install, tests, artifact build, container image creation, staged deployment, health checks, rollback criteria, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Review my Terraform workflow for production readiness.
output: Module boundaries and environment separation to verify.
State storage, locking, and access control checks.
Plan review, policy validation, and rollback considerations.

output_examples.input: Prepare deployment notes for a Kubernetes service.
output_examples.output: A runbook outline with preflight checks, manifest review, rollout monitoring, failure signals, rollback steps, and post-deployment verification.

best_practices: Review generated commands before running them against local, staging, or production systems.
Run helper scripts in a controlled project directory and choose output paths deliberately.
Validate all infrastructure recommendations against team policies and cloud provider requirements.
anti_patterns: Do not run deployment commands directly in production without review and approval.
Do not treat placeholder reference examples as complete production templates.
Do not write script output to sensitive files or shared configuration paths.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy applications automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, examples, and simple helper scripts. Users must run and approve deployment actions.

faq.question: Can it create complete Terraform modules?
faq.answer: No. The Terraform material is reference guidance. The scripts currently produce simple analysis reports.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it access cloud credentials?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the scripts read credentials or environment secrets.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The scripts can write report files to user-provided paths, and the documentation includes operational command examples.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Platform engineers, infrastructure engineers, and team leads who need DevOps planning support.
